stories.title: Knicks 144-114 76ers Game 4 — New York Sweeps Philadelphia Behind McBride's Record Quarter

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The #NewYorkKnicks are in the Eastern Conference Finals.

Deuce McBride: 25 pts, 7-for-9 from three — hit 4 3s IN THE FIRST QUARTER.

That's the first time a Knick has done that in a playoff game since 1997. 144-114. Sweep. #NewYorkForever

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
25 3-pointers. 11 in the first quarter alone (ties an NBA playoff record).

Brunson 22. Hart 17. KAT 17. McBride 25.

The #Knicks won their 7th double-digit playoff game this postseason. They are LOCKED IN. #NewYorkForever

facebook.long_form:
The New York Knicks are going to the Eastern Conference Finals.

On Sunday afternoon, New York dismantled Philadelphia 144-114 in Game 4 at Wells Fargo Center, completing a stunning 4-0 sweep and booking their ticket to the conference finals for the first time since their run to the ECF in recent years. It was their seventh double-digit playoff win of the 2026 postseason.

The story of the afternoon was Miles "Deuce" McBride. Jalen Brunson and Karl-Anthony Towns got the headlines all series, but it was the reserve guard who stole the show Sunday. McBride came off the bench and erupted for 25 points on 7-of-9 shooting from three — a career playoff high. Four of those three-pointers came in the first quarter alone, making him the first Knick since play-by-play tracking began in 1997 to hit four threes in a single quarter of a playoff game.

That first quarter was something to behold. New York hit 11 three-pointers in those first 12 minutes, tying the NBA playoff record for three-pointers in a quarter. By the time the fog of that first-quarter barrage cleared, the game was essentially over.

Brunson added 22 points, Josh Hart and KAT each contributed 17, and the Knicks simply overwhelmed a Philadelphia team that has been outclassed all series. Joel Embiid, fighting through injury all postseason, couldn't stop the bleeding. The final margin was 30 points.

The Knicks will wait for the winner of the Pistons-Cavaliers series, which could be decided as soon as tonight. This New York team looks like a genuine contender for the championship.
facebook.image_caption: Deuce McBride: 25 pts, 4 3s in ONE quarter. The Knicks swept the 76ers 4-0 and are headed to the ECF. New York is LOCKED IN. #NewYorkForever

article.headline: Deuce McBride's Record Quarter Fuels Knicks' 144-114 Demolition of 76ers, Completing the Sweep

stories.title: Wembanyama's First Career Ejection Fails to Stop Wolves — Minnesota Rallies to Win 114-109, Series Tied 2-2

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Wembanyama ejected early in Q2 — Flagrant 2 for elbowing Naz Reid in the face.

The earliest an All-Star has been tossed in a playoff game since 1997.

Edwards dropped 36 (16 in Q4). Wolves rallied from 8 down. Series tied 2-2. #Timberwolves

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
San Antonio led by 8 in the fourth quarter.

Then Anthony Edwards happened. 16 pts in Q4. Wolves outscored the Spurs 28-15 in the final 8:51.

Final: #Timberwolves 114, Spurs 109. Series tied 2-2. Game 5 in San Antonio.

facebook.long_form:
The 2026 playoffs delivered their most dramatic night yet on Sunday, and it had nothing to do with the final score.

Victor Wembanyama — the reigning Defensive Player of the Year and the face of the San Antonio Spurs' rebuild — was ejected from Game 4 of the Western Conference Semifinals early in the second quarter after elbowing Minnesota's Naz Reid in the face and neck. After a video review, referee Zach Zarba upgraded the call to a Flagrant 2, automatically disqualifying Wembanyama for the remainder of the game. He finished with 4 points in just 13 minutes.

According to ESPN Research, it was the earliest an All-Star had been ejected from a playoff game since play-by-play tracking began in 1997. Target Center was electric, fans chanting "Kick him out!" as the call was reviewed.

And yet — the Spurs nearly won anyway. San Antonio led by 8 points in the fourth quarter, seemingly on course to take a commanding 3-1 series lead despite losing their star. Then Anthony Edwards happened.

The 24-year-old Wolves superstar scored 16 of his 36 points in the final quarter, turning a Minnesota deficit into a 28-15 closing run over the last 8:51 of the game. Edwards finished 13-of-22 from the field, 3-of-5 from three, and 7-of-8 from the free-throw line — a complete and dominant performance.

The final score was Minnesota 114, San Antonio 109. The series is tied at 2-2. Game 5 heads back to San Antonio, where the Spurs will hope to have Wembanyama and the crowd advantage working in their favor.

Wembanyama's ejection also creates a potential cloud over Game 5: the Flagrant 2 automatically places him under enhanced review, and the NBA's Basketball Operations department could add additional flagrant points that would trigger an automatic suspension.
facebook.image_caption: Wemby ejected (Flagrant 2), Edwards erupts for 36 (16 in Q4). The Wolves rally from 8 down to win 114-109. Series tied 2-2. Game 5 in San Antonio. #Timberwolves

stories.title: Pistons vs. Cavaliers Game 4 Tonight — Cleveland Eyes Series Tie at Home

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Game 4 TONIGHT — #Pistons at #Cavs, 8:00 PM ET on NBC.

Detroit leads 2-1. Cleveland answered with Mitchell's 35-pt game.

Win tonight = series tied 2-2. Lose = down 3-1 with the season on the line.

facebook.long_form:
The Eastern Conference Semifinals between Detroit and Cleveland are far from over.

The Pistons arrived in this series as the surprise team, winning Games 1 and 2 on the road before Cleveland finally answered with a 116-109 home win in Game 3 behind Donovan Mitchell's 35-point masterpiece and his 2,000th career playoff point.

Tonight, they go back to Cleveland for Game 4 at 8:00 PM ET on NBC. The stakes are clear: a Cavs win ties the series 2-2; a Pistons win puts Detroit in control at 3-1 with a potential series close at home.

Cade Cunningham has been the engine of Detroit's run — 23 points and 7 assists in Game 1, 25 points and 10 assists in Game 2 (including 12 in the fourth quarter on 8-of-8 from the free throw line). He makes Detroit go in ways we haven't seen from young Pistons players in years.

But Cleveland has the crowd, the momentum, and Mitchell locked in. This is one of the most watchable series left in the bracket. Tonight's game could define the entire series.

Tune in at 8:00 PM ET on NBC/Peacock.
facebook.image_caption: Detroit leads 2-1 but Cleveland answers back. Game 4 TONIGHT at 8 PM ET on NBC. Can the Cavs even it up or do the Pistons take control? #Cavs #Pistons

stories.title: Thunder vs. Lakers Game 4 Tonight — OKC One Win From Sweep

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Oklahoma City is 7-0 in the 2026 playoffs. They lead the #LakeShow 3-0.

Game 4 is TONIGHT at 10:30 PM ET on Prime Video.

The Lakers need three straight wins against this team. Without Luka. The math is brutal. #ThunderUp

facebook.long_form:
Oklahoma City has not lost a game in the 2026 NBA Playoffs. They are 7-0. They lead the Los Angeles Lakers 3-0. Tonight, they go to Los Angeles for Game 4 with the chance to sweep one of basketball's most storied franchises.

The Thunder have won by margins of 18, 18, and 23 points in this series. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has been brilliant, Chet Holmgren dominant, and the depth has been overwhelming. Meanwhile, the Lakers are playing without Luka Doncic, who remains sidelined with a Grade 2 hamstring strain and is still not cleared for contact.

LeBron James has done everything he can. He's averaging 21 points in the series, but his Game 3 showing — 7-of-19 from the field — shows even he is struggling to manufacture advantages against this defense. Rui Hachimura led the Lakers in scoring in Game 3 with 21 points. Austin Reaves has been game, but it hasn't been enough.

No NBA team has ever come back from an 0-3 series deficit. In 150-plus attempts, it has never happened. Tonight, Oklahoma City has the chance to add another clean sweep to a historic run.

Game 4: Oklahoma City at Los Angeles, 10:30 PM ET, Prime Video.
facebook.image_caption: Oklahoma City is 7-0 and leads 3-0. The Lakers need a miracle. Game 4 TONIGHT at 10:30 PM ET on Prime. Can LeBron keep the season alive? #ThunderUp #LakeShow

stories.title: Bracket Update — Knicks Waiting, Wembanyama Suspension Watch, WCF Taking Shape

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Where we stand in the 2026 NBA Playoffs:

✅ Knicks: In the ECF, waiting
⚔️ Pistons-Cavs: Tied 1-2, G4 tonight
⚔️ Thunder-Lakers: OKC leads 3-0, G4 tonight
⚔️ Spurs-Wolves: Tied 2-2, G5 in San Antonio

Who's your pick to make the Finals? #NBA

facebook.long_form:
Let's take a breath and look at where the 2026 NBA Playoffs stand.

The New York Knicks are already in the Eastern Conference Finals. They swept the 76ers in four games and are now the waiting power — rested, sharp, and watching to see who comes out of the Detroit-Cleveland series.

In the East, the Pistons-Cavaliers series resumes tonight at 8 PM ET in Cleveland. Detroit leads 2-1. If Cleveland wins tonight, it's 2-2 with all the momentum shifting to the Cavs. If Detroit wins, it's 3-1 with the Pistons on the brink of closing it out.

In the West, it's a matter of when — not if — the Thunder advance. Oklahoma City leads the Lakers 3-0 and plays tonight at 10:30 PM ET. The other Western series — Spurs vs. Timberwolves — just got a lot more interesting after Wembanyama's ejection and the Wolves' comeback to tie it 2-2. Game 5 is in San Antonio, and Wembanyama could face suspension. That series is wide open.

The bracket is shaping up for a compelling final two rounds. Who's your pick to win it all?
facebook.image_caption: Knicks: ECF ✅ | Thunder-Lakers G4 tonight | Pistons-Cavs G4 tonight | Spurs-Wolves tied 2-2. The 2026 playoff bracket is getting real. #NBA
